text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Hawthorn Hollow, a golden sunbeam danced through the branches of the towering walnut tree on the corner of Main Street. It tickled the surface of Lucy, the chatty rooster, who was perched on an ancient branch.

"Ahem!" Lucy sighed. "What would you fellows be scheming today?"

Her squawks were cut short by the arrival of three little robins. They chirped gleefully. "Oh, we're just plotting!" one piped up. "We're gonna build the biggest, most beautiful nest on all the trees!"

Lucy laughed heartily. "Building a nest? Oh, that sounds too easy! Why don't you try putting some twine around some wildflowers?"

The robins flew down, collecting twine. Lucy watched enviously as they danced through cherry blossoms.

"Wait!" called a third robin. "Don't forget feathers! My mother's nest has the most luxurious down
